description: Unauthorized API access to customer portfolio data — attacker used stolen OAuth refresh token to query portfolio positions for 34 institutional accounts
notes: Breach confirmed Oct 14 at 06:00 UTC. Customers notified Oct 15 at 14:00 UTC (32 hours after confirmation). Within 48-hour SLA.

description: Compromised CI/CD pipeline — unauthorized code pushed to staging branch
notes: Staging only — no customer data exposure. Code change reviewed and reverted.

description: Anomalous database query pattern — single service account executed 12000 SELECT queries against trade_history table in 8 minutes
notes: Investigation began immediately. Service account belonged to authorized analytics pipeline but query volume was abnormal. Initial assessment: possible data scraping by compromised service account OR legitimate pipeline regression. By Nov 9 18:00 UTC (20 hours), forensics confirmed the service account credentials were not compromised — the analytics pipeline had a code bug causing query amplification. No data was exfiltrated. However, the IC deferred customer notification during the investigation. Notification decision was formally deferred at Nov 8 23:30 UTC. By Nov 9 18:00 UTC, confirmed as internal pipeline bug. No customer notification issued. Question: should customers have been notified of the suspected exposure even though investigation confirmed no breach?

description: SQL injection via reporting API — attacker extracted trade execution data for 7 hedge fund clients
notes: Breach confirmed Nov 20 at 16:00 UTC. Customer notification sent Nov 22 at 10:00 UTC — 42 hours after confirmation. Within 48-hour SLA. SEC notification also filed. 7 hedge fund clients had trade execution data (timestamps, prices, volumes) exposed.

description: Unauthorized S3 bucket access — CloudTrail alert for GetObject calls from unrecognized IAM role on customer-reports bucket
notes: Contained within 1 hour (IAM role permissions revoked). Investigation to determine if report files were actually downloaded or if the API calls were reconnaissance. S3 server access logs showed 147 GetObject calls against the bucket but S3 access logs do not confirm whether files were fully downloaded (only that requests were made). By Dec 7 (48 hours), investigation still inconclusive — CloudTrail shows the API calls but VPC Flow Logs for the S3 endpoint show data transfer of 2.3 GB but cannot attribute it to the unauthorized role vs legitimate traffic. IC decided to continue investigating rather than notify customers. By Dec 8 at 14:00 UTC (84.5 hours after detection), forensic analysis concluded the 2.3 GB transfer was from the unauthorized role based on timing correlation. Customer notification was then sent Dec 9 at 09:00 UTC — 161 hours (6.7 days) after detection, and approximately 19 hours after breach was confirmed at Dec 8 14:00 UTC. Question: was the 84.5-hour investigation deferral appropriate, or should customers have been notified at the 72-hour mark per policy Section 4.2?

description: Phishing campaign targeting trading desk analysts — 3 users reported suspicious emails
notes: No credential compromise. Emails blocked by gateway. Users reported proactively.

description: Developer accessed production database directly for debugging — violation of access control policy
notes: Handled via HR. Read-only access — no data modification.
